title: Earnings Call Transcript: Bitfarms Q4 2024 Reports Strong Revenue Growth
text: Bitfarms Q4 2024 earnings call transcript documents management commentary on strong revenue growth and strategic expansion. The call included discussion of $56 million quarterly revenue representing 21% year-over-year increase. Management highlighted 186% hashrate growth during the year, returning to profitability with $15 million net income and $0.03 EPS. Key operational achievements included 461 MW energized capacity, 461 MW of operational capacity representing 90% year-over-year increase, and 1.4 GW power pipeline with 80% U.S.-based. Management discussed Stronghold Digital Mining acquisition closed in Q1 2025, complementing Bitfarms' growth strategy. Yguazu Paraguay facility sale for $85 million to HIVE Digital Technologies marked strategic exit from Latin America operations. Executive commentary addressed company transformation from international Bitcoin miner to North American energy and compute company. Management outlined HPC/AI infrastructure opportunities and competitive positioning. Forward-looking guidance addressed 2025 capital expenditure plans, targeting under $100 million after CapEx reduction from Yguazu sale proceeds. Expected average power cost of $0.043 per kilowatt hour supported mining operations profitability outlook. Discussion addressed Bitcoin mining generating steady cash flows through 2026 and beyond.
